text:
There was an old man living on an island. Every morning, he ate berries ripe from a peculiar tree. One day, while climbing the tallest branch, he fell. Instead of landing awkwardly, he drifted gently downwards. Landing atop a mushroom.

The man thought nothing of it; mushrooms were his friends. As he nibbled on a delicious-looking specimen, an owl soared past. The man chuckled softly. An hour passed; nothing unusual. Then stars erupted overhead.

A parade of nocturnal creatures drifted by, and the old man wasn’t alone. He had an audience. Owls, owlets, bats—not scary at all. They flew around him, curious.

Then the old man felt an odd prickling behind his ears. Nocturnal isn’t scary. That’s just his imagination. He fell back asleep.

When morning came, the old man shook his head. Maybe it wasn’t just an imagination thing. He gathered his berries.

But then he saw them—a few tiny foot
